description: Ah Kong was a Singaporean organized crime syndicate that controlled the European heroin trade from the 1970s to the 1990s. Based in Amsterdam and Bangkok, it was one of the world's largest drug syndicates, sourcing heroin from the Golden Triangle. The gang was formed by Singaporean Chinese who fled to the Netherlands after a gang clash. It declined after its boss was assassinated in 1997 and the last official boss died in 2010.

country_links.evidence_quote: They are scattered all over Europe, Asia, and Australia; and some others have joined other groups.
country_links.context: Ah Kong members are scattered in Australia.

country_links.evidence_quote: Siam-kia has made an enormous windfall from real estate investments in Cambodia and runs a casino there.
country_links.context: Ah Kong leader Siam-kia made windfall from real estate investments and runs a casino there.

country_links.evidence_quote: Roland Tan owned Restaurant Bali, a restaurant serving Indonesian and Singaporean food, and a few other restaurants in Copenhagen.
country_links.context: Ah Kong member Roland Tan owned businesses and was active in Denmark.

country_links.evidence_quote: Ah Kong flew its couriers to 'safer' airports in London, Oslo, Copenhagen, Paris and Rome.
country_links.context: Ah Kong flew couriers to Paris as a safer airport.
